title: Blender Beginner Basics: Moving, Modes, and First Model

steps.hint: Scroll the middle mouse wheel to zoom in and out
steps.narration: On your mouse, your middle scroll wheel will let you zoom out and zoom in.

steps.hint: Click and drag the middle scroll wheel to orbit around your object
steps.narration: If you click in the middle scroll wheel and drag around, you can go around your object in whatever way you're bringing your mouse.

steps.hint: Hold Shift and click the middle scroll wheel to pan around your scene
steps.narration: If you hold shift and click the middle scroll wheel, you can pan around your scene.

steps.hint: Press '1' on the number pad for front orthographic view
steps.narration: If you have a number pad, you can click one for front orthographic.

steps.hint: Press '3' on the number pad for right orthographic view
steps.narration: Three for right orthographic

steps.hint: Press '7' on the number pad for top orthographic view
steps.narration: seven for top

steps.hint: Press '9' on the number pad for bottom orthographic view
steps.narration: and nine for bottom.

steps.hint: Hold Alt, click the middle mouse wheel, and drag to snap to views without a number pad
steps.narration: If you don't have a number pad, you can hold Alt on your keyboard and click in your middle mouse wheel and drag in the direction you want it to be.

steps.hint: Click on the cube object
steps.narration: If we click on our object, this cube

steps.hint: Click the Modes dropdown menu
steps.narration: and then go up to our modes here

steps.hint: Press 'G' to grab (move) the object
steps.narration: So, let's say we press G to grab it.

steps.hint: Click the vertex selection mode button
steps.narration: If you click on the verticy selection mode

steps.hint: Click any vertex on your mesh
steps.narration: you can click any vert on your mesh

steps.hint: Press 'G' to grab the selected vertex
steps.narration: If we press G to grab

steps.hint: Drag the selected vertex to move it
steps.narration: we can move this in.

steps.hint: Click the edge selection mode button
steps.narration: If we want to grab a line to move it all at once

steps.hint: Click an edge on the mesh
steps.narration: grab a line

steps.hint: Click the face selection mode button
steps.narration: And if we want to grab a face

steps.hint: Click a face on the mesh
steps.narration: grab a face

steps.hint: Press Ctrl+Z to undo
steps.narration: Let's press Ctrl + Z to get back to a basic cube

steps.hint: Press 'A' to select everything within the mesh
steps.narration: and I press A to select everything within this mesh.

steps.hint: Press 'S' to scale your object
steps.narration: Up next is S. If you press S on your keyboard, you can scale your object down.

steps.hint: Press 'E' to extrude a face
steps.narration: Up next, we have E to extrude.

steps.hint: Press 'R' to rotate the object
steps.narration: Now, we could press R to rotate.

steps.hint: Press 'R' then 'Y' to rotate only on the Y-axis
steps.narration: So if you press R and then Y, for example, you are only rotating on the Y ais.

steps.hint: Press 'S' then 'X' to scale only on the X-axis
steps.narration: So we could do scale in the X-axis, for example.

steps.hint: Press Ctrl+R to add loop cuts
steps.narration: CtrlR adds loop cuts

steps.hint: Scroll the middle mouse wheel to add more loop cut segments
steps.narration: and we could scroll on our mouse if we want more.

steps.hint: Click to confirm the loop cut placement
steps.narration: But for now, we're just going to click to add that one.

steps.hint: Press 'S' to scale the new loop cut
steps.narration: Then we could press scale

steps.hint: Right-click to open the context menu
steps.narration: And sure, we could rightclick

steps.hint: Click 'Shade Smooth'
steps.narration: and do shade smooth

steps.hint: Right-click to open the context menu
steps.narration: We could rightclick

steps.element: Shade Smooth Auto Smooth menu item
steps.hint: Click 'Shade Smooth Auto Smooth'
steps.narration: do shade smooth, auto smooth

steps.hint: Hold Alt and click an edge to select an edge loop
steps.narration: If we hold alt and select this line

steps.hint: Press Ctrl+B to bevel the selected edge
steps.narration: We could do controll andb to bevel this.

steps.hint: Scroll the middle mouse wheel to add more bevel segments
steps.narration: We can also use our scroll wheel to add more segments

steps.hint: Right-click to open the context menu
steps.narration: we could just shade smooth, auto smooth

steps.element: Shade Smooth Auto Smooth menu item
steps.hint: Click 'Shade Smooth Auto Smooth'
steps.narration: shade smooth, auto smooth

steps.hint: Press 'S' to scale the object down
steps.narration: I'm going to press S to scale it down.

steps.hint: Press 'Delete' to remove the object
steps.narration: Delete.

steps.hint: Press Shift+A to open the Add menu
steps.narration: If you press shift and A on your keyboard, it brings up this add menu.

steps.hint: Click 'Mesh' in the Add menu
steps.narration: I am in this mesh subsection.

steps.hint: Click 'Cylinder' to add a cylinder
steps.narration: We're going to bring in this cylinder right here.

steps.hint: Click the arrow to expand the Add Cylinder options
steps.narration: We could click this arrow down here

steps.hint: Type '8' into the Vertices count field
steps.narration: I'm going to put it at eight.

steps.hint: Press 'Tab' to switch to Edit Mode
steps.narration: The shortcut for this would be tab on your keyboard to switch back and forth.

steps.hint: Press 'S' to scale the cylinder down
steps.narration: But in edit mode, let's scale this down.

steps.hint: Click the top face of the cylinder
steps.narration: Grab our top face

steps.hint: Drag the selected face up
steps.narration: and drag it up.

steps.hint: Press 'S' to scale the top face
steps.narration: Press S to scale.

steps.hint: Right-click to open the context menu
steps.narration: We could rightclick

steps.element: Shade Smooth Auto Smooth menu item
steps.hint: Click 'Shade Smooth Auto Smooth'
steps.narration: shade smooth auto smooth.

steps.hint: Adjust the Auto Smooth Angle setting
steps.narration: We can also up this angle so it actually looks a bit smoother.

steps.hint: Press Shift+A to open the Add menu again
steps.narration: Let's do shift A again.

steps.hint: Click 'Circle' to add a circle
steps.narration: And let's add in let's do a circle cuz why not?

steps.hint: Type '12' into the Vertices count field for the circle
steps.narration: And we'll make this 12 sides.

steps.hint: Press 'Tab' to switch to Edit Mode
steps.narration: Now let's tab into edit mode once again.

steps.hint: Click the edge selection mode button
steps.narration: Go to our line select mode
